claim: We conclude that in the field of public education the doctrine of "separate but equal" has no place.
notes: Seed claim; keep citations attached when summarizing.

claim: Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal.
notes: Seed claim; keep citations attached when summarizing.

claim: Segregation of children in public schools solely on the basis of race deprives the children of the minority group of equal educational opportunities.
notes: Seed claim; keep citations attached when summarizing.

claim: To separate children solely because of their race generates a feeling of inferiority that may affect their hearts and minds in a way unlikely ever to be undone.
notes: Seed claim; keep citations attached when summarizing.

claim: Any language in Plessy v. Ferguson contrary to the Court’s finding on segregation’s effects in education is rejected.
notes: Seed claim; keep citations attached when summarizing.
